data.title: Valero Energy downgraded at Bank of America on valuation
data.description:
Bank of America on Thursday downgraded Valero Energy (VLO) to Neutral from a previous investment rating of Buy as part of its 2026 outlook for the oil and gas sector, warning that the refiner’s risk-reward profile has weakened after a strong year and amid growing uncertainty around crack spreads and geopolitical developments.

In its newly published Integrateds, Refining and Midstream: Year Ahead 2026 report, the firm said it now sees Valero’s (VLO) valuation as less compelling, especially if a potential Russia-Ukraine peace agreement restores lost Russian refining capacity and narrows margins. BofA trimmed its price objective to $195, noting that even in a “no peace” scenario, free-cash-flow yields still fall short of levels that justify additional upside. In a “peace” scenario, the bank estimates FCF yield could sink toward 5.5%, down sharply from expectations earlier in the year.

BofA analysts highlighted that refiners benefited from an unusual combination of supportive factors in 2025, including drone strikes on Russian infrastructure, elevated European gas prices, and slow ramp-ups of global refinery additions. Those forces may dissipate in 2026. The report identifies Valero as particularly exposed because it is a pure-play refiner, and its stock has already priced in optimistic heavy crude discount trends.

The downgrade came as BofA outlined a broader sector view shaped by oversupplied oil markets, lower projected Brent crude prices ($60 in 2026), and potential shifts in LNG flows if Russian gas returns to Europe. The bank maintains positive views on several LNG and midstream names but sees refiners at elevated risk should crack spreads weaken more than expected.

While BofA still views Valero favorably relative to its refining peers due to its leverage to widening heavy crude discounts, the analysts said the stock’s recent rally, combined with heightened geopolitical sensitivity, leaves limited room for further appreciation.

data.title: Tech Voices: Disney teams up with OpenAI, Rivian's new chip
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • Disney (DIS) said it has agreed to invest $1 billion in OpenAI (OPENAI) along with allowing its characters to be used in fan videos generated by OpenAI's Sora app.

Under the agreement, Disney will grant a three-year license to Sora that will allow users to access 200 Disney characters to create short videos. The deal does not cover any Disney character voices or talent likeness. 

In addition to its $1 billion investment, Disney will also receive warrants to buy additional equity and become an OpenAI customer, according to the companies. 

“Technological innovation has continually shaped the evolution of entertainment, bringing with it new ways to create and share great stories with the world,” Disney CEO Robert Iger said in the statement. 

“The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence marks an important moment for our industry, and through this collaboration with OpenAI we will thoughtfully and responsibly extend the reach of our storytelling through generative AI, while respecting and protecting creators and their works," Iger added.

OpenAI, which was co-founded by Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk, counts Microsoft (MSFT) as a major backer.

  • Rivian (RIVN) said it's developed its own chip to replace Nvidia's (NVDA) as part of an effort to equip its vehicles with enhanced autonomous driving capabilities. 

The EV maker will equip its new R2 SUVs with Rivian Autonomy Processor 1 chips, along with a new lidar sensor and AI model developments. The chips will be produced by Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM). 

“This is not a bet one takes lightly, this is a huge commitment that’s taken us years,” Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe told Bloomberg. “Usually, you can’t lower cost and improve performance. But here, we improved performance dramatically and simultaneously lowered cost by hundreds of dollars per vehicle.”

Starting in 2027, Rivian will begin offering software aimed at allowing drivers to operate the vehicle without needing to keep their hands on the wheel and eyes on the road, Bloomberg said.

data.title: FTC is said to weigh antitrust objection to Henkel purchase of glue maker
data.description:
The U.S. Federal Trade Commission is gearing up to challenge a planned acquisition by Henkel (HENKY) (HENOY) (HELKF) involving part of Pittsburgh Paint Co., a business controlled by private-equity firm American Industrial Partners, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The deal, still unannounced, would bring together two major suppliers of liquid construction adhesives, the people said, speaking on condition of anonymity because the details remain confidential. Henkel, the global leader in adhesives and maker of the Loctite and LePage brands, would acquire a business that includes Liquid Nails, one of the best-known products in the category.

The FTC could file its complaint as early as this week, the people said.

The move marks the second ownership change for the Liquid Nails brand in less than a year. PPG Industries (PPG) sold its US and Canadian architectural coatings operations to AIP for $550 million in late 2024, a transaction that restored the business’s historic name, Pittsburgh Paint.

Henkel generated €11 billion ($12.9 billion) in 2024 sales from its adhesives division, more than half of the company’s total revenue, according to its most recent annual report. Pittsburgh Paint, meanwhile, posted roughly $2 billion in 2023 sales before being spun out of PPG.

Under Chair Andrew Ferguson, who took over the FTC in January, the agency has already attempted to block two other transactions. Regulators challenged a merger involving medical-device coating suppliers earlier this year and recently completed a court fight over Edwards Lifesciences’ planned acquisition of a competitor, with a decision still pending.

data.title: As Dow notches record high, here are all 30 components ranked by SA Quant
data.description:
The blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) surged to a fresh intraday record high on Thursday, extending its prominent leadership role in U.S. equities.

The index jumped as much as 698.47 points to reach 48,756.34, its highest level on record, as investors continued rotating into large-cap industrial and cyclical names. With the milestone in sight, traders are now speculating whether the Dow could next challenge the 50,000 mark—roughly 2.5% above current levels.

The Dow is +14.2% YTD and +2.8% the past month, underscoring persistent momentum despite any chatter about market uncertainty.

As the benchmark pushes deeper into record territory, Seeking Alpha identified which Dow components currently earn the highest marks under its Quant Rating framework.

Here are all 30 Dow stocks ranked by their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings:

  1. Merck (MRK), Quant Rating: 4.82, Strong Buy.

data.title: As Rivian touts LiDAR integration plans, SA Quant ranks the biggest names in the sector
data.description:
Rivian (RIVN) on Thursday said it was planning to integrate light detection and ranging (LiDAR) technology into future car models, putting the sector in the spotlight.

LiDAR uses laser pulses for detection and ranging and is installed in cars to help with the mapping of surroundings. Rivian (RIVN) said the technology will provide "detailed, three-dimensional spatial data" and improve "real-time detection for the edge cases of driving."

Luminar Technologies (LAZR) was a company mentioned by Rivian (RIVN), sending class A shares of the LiDAR maker up +15.6%.

Including LAZR, Seeking Alpha took a look at major LiDAR manufacturers and ranked them by their Quant ratings. Luminar (LAZR) landed at the bottom of the list with a Strong Sell rating of 1.33.

AEye (LIDR) topped the list with a Hold rating of 3.38, followed by Ouster (OUST) with a Hold rating of 3.19. MicroVision (MVIS) and Hesai (HSAI) are tied with Hold ratings of 3.03, while Aeva Technologies (AEVA) rounds out the top five with a Hold rating of 3.02. The list also includes Innoviz Technologies (INVZ) and indie Semiconductor (INDI).

Note that Semilux (SELX) and Valeo (VLEEY) are not covered by Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system.

For reference, SA’s Quant system ranks stocks based on their performance on certain critical quantitative measures, such as valuation, growth, and profitability. Each stock is rated on a scale of 1 to 5, with any rating above 3.5 indicating a bullish rating. A score of 2.5 or below represents a bearish profile.

Here is the list:

  1. AEye (LIDR), Quant Rating: 3.38, Hold.
  2. Ouster (OUST), Quant Rating: 3.19, Hold.
  3. MicroVision (MVIS), Quant Rating: 3.03, Hold.
  4. Hesai Group (HSAI), Quant Rating: 3.03, Hold.
  5. Aeva Technologies (AEVA), Quant Rating: 3.02, Hold.
  6. Innoviz Technologies (INVZ), Quant Rating: 2.68, Hold.
  7. indie Semiconductor (INDI), Quant Rating: 2.63, Hold.
  8. Luminar Technologies (LAZR), Quant Rating: 1.33, Strong Sell.  

Here are some semiconductor-focused exchange-traded funds: (SMH), (SOXX), (SOXL), (FTXL), (XSD), (USD), (PSI), and (SEMI).

data.title: Brazil output swings, OPEC outlook diverge as 2026 market forecasts split
data.description:
Brazil’s sharp oil-production swings are colliding with OPEC’s insistence that the crude market will remain broadly balanced next year, underscoring how unpredictable supply from major non-OPEC producers is complicating the Saudi-led group’s efforts to manage global flows.

New preliminary data from Brazil’s oil regulator ANP show the country’s crude output fell about 8% in November to roughly 3.7 million barrels a day after several offshore platforms including the massive Búzios field were taken offline.

The outages temporarily erased more than 300,000 barrels a day from Latin America’s largest producer, a reminder of how the shift toward “super platforms,” each capable of producing more than 200,000 barrels a day, can amplify month-to-month volatility.

Some facilities have since restarted, with ANP figures indicating roughly one-fifth of the lost output had returned by late last week. Analysts say the dip does little to alter Brazil’s longer-term trajectory, which remains upward alongside other regional growth engines like Guyana and Argentina. Still, the sizable month-to-month swings highlight how difficult it is for traders and policymakers to interpret near-term supply trends.

OPEC sees supply aligned with demand

Those uncertainties arrive just as OPEC releases an outlook that diverges sharply from other major forecasters. The producer group left its projections for 2026 unchanged, arguing that global supply and demand will be broadly aligned next year. According to its latest report, OPEC and its partners would need to pump around 43 million barrels a day to keep the market balanced—roughly the volumes recorded last month.

That assessment stands in contrast to warnings from the International Energy Agency and trading house Trafigura, both of which expect a significant oversupply. The IEA still sees a sizeable surplus emerging in 2026 despite trimming its estimate this week, while Trafigura cautioned that the market could be headed for a “super glut.”

Acknowledging the uncertain backdrop, key OPEC+ members agreed last month to halt further production increases during the first quarter of 2026 after rapidly ramping up earlier in the year. Skeptics note that the group’s forecasts have leaned too optimistic in recent years: OPEC had to slash demand projections multiple times last year and abandoned expectations for a major stockpile drawdown that never appeared.

For now, Brazil’s rebound from November’s outages and OPEC’s steady outlook offer opposing snapshots of a market in flux, one shaped as much by unpredictable operational swings in non-OPEC supply as by policy decisions in Riyadh and Vienna.

data.title: Trump-tied WLFI adds USD1 trading pairs on Binance crypto exchange
data.description:
Crypto exchange Binance is making Trump-linked stablecoin USD1 a core part of its infrastructure just months after launch, according to a Thursday release.

Binance users can now trade BNB/USD1, ETH/USD1 and SOL/USD1, bringing World Liberty Financial's ("WLFI") dollar-backed stablecoin into some of Binance's most active spot markets.

In addition to the trading pairs, all of Binance's collateral assets backing Binance's BUSD-pegged token (B-Token) will be converted into USD1 at a one-to-one ratio, a process anticipated to be completed within a week.

“Binance’s expansion of USD1 marks an important moment in WLFI’s effort to make digital US dollar stablecoins available to people everywhere,” said Zach Witkoff, CEO and co-founder of World Liberty Financial. “By integrating USD1 into liquidity, trading, and collateral systems on the world’s largest exchange, Binance is giving hundreds of millions of users improved access to USD1.”

USD1 is supported by reserves held in short-dated U.S. Treasury bills, cash deposits and other cash equivalents. 

WLFI is the decentralized finance ("DeFi)" protocol and governance platform linked to the Trump family. In October, U.S. President Donald Trump pardoned Changpeng Zhao, the convicted founder Binance who was prosecuted under the Biden administration for violating anti-money laundering rules

data.title: Anheuser-Busch InBev announces brewery closings amid production shift to other facilities
data.description:
Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) announced that it will close its Merrimack, New Hampshire brewery facility in early 2026 as part of efforts to modernize its U.S. infrastructure. The Merrimack plant, which has been operational for about 50 years, is set to be shuttered by January 31, 2026. This move aligns with closures at other sites, including Fairfield, California, and the sale of its Newark, New Jersey brewery

A spokesperson with Anheuser-Busch (BUD) confirmed that the beer giant will be shifting production from the three facilities to other U.S. facilities. "These changes will enable us to invest even more in our remaining operations and in our portfolio of growing, industry-leading brands," stated the spokesperson.

Currently, Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) brands hold a leading position in the U.S. beer market, with about 33% overall market share. Michelob Ultra has emerged as the top-selling beer by volume nationwide, while Bud Light ranks third. Meanwhile, Busch Light ranks as America's second fastest-growing beer in 2025. Anheuser-Busch (BUD) also leads craft market share gains among top manufacturers, fueled by IPA brands.

data.title: Allete gets written order approving acquisition from MPUC, deal close Dec. 15
data.description:
Allete (ALE) received a written order from the Minnesota Public Utilities Commission approving its sale to Canada Pension Plan Investment Board and Global Infrastructure Partners.

The acquisition is now expected to close on Monday, according to a statement on Thursday. The deal has also been approved by Allete (ALE) shareholders and federal and state agencies, including the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission and the Public Service Commission of Wisconsin.

MPUC commissioners '  approved the planned sale to  CPPIB and BlackRock's (BLK) Global Infrastructure Partners in October.

CPP Investments and GIP announced in May 2024 that they would acquire Allete (NYSE:ALE) for $67/share in cash, or $6.2B including debt.

data.title: Biohaven jumps on early-stage data for antibody drug conjugate combo
data.description:
• Biohaven (BHVN) is up ~8% in Thursday trading after reporting phase 1 data on a combination of its Trop2 antibody drug conjugate BHV-1510 with Regeneron Pharmaceuticals' (REGN) Libtayo (cemiplimab) across several tumor types.
  • Enrolled patients had prior treatment, mostly with PD-L1 inhibitors. Data showed that among the 23 efficacy evaluable patients, treatment with BHV-1510 2.5 mg/kg plus Libtayo every three weeks led to an overall confirmed objective response rate of 52%. The rates were 42.9% in non-small cell lung cancer, 66.7% in endometrial cancer, and 50% in urothelial cancer.
  • Biohaven noted that no cases of interstitial lung disease were observed, a potential safety differentiating factor for BHV-1510 compared to other Trop2 ADCs. About 23% of patients experienced Grade 3 or higher oral mucositis/stomatitis toxicity, though this is a known class effect.
  • Results were presented at the 2025 European Society for Medical Oncology  Immuno-Oncology Congress.
